summary: Good soundboard capture of a memorable Rosemont night with a sweet short Dark Star and standout acoustic Looks Like Rain, despite some first‑set interference.
review: The show is praised for its vocal energy from Jerry, a rare Chicago Scarlet/Fire, an acoustic rendition of Looks Like Rain and a concise but lovely Dark Star that lifts the performance. Recording-wise the 3D restoration has produced a generally clear soundboard source, though listeners note PA/FM interference on the first set. Overall the concert is considered one of the better early‑90s Chicago dates.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating ~4.5 (show quality) and recording noted as good with some interference, yielding a weighted score of about 4.2.

recording_quality.technical_notes: First‑set marred by PA/FM interference, but 3D restoration makes the overall mix clear and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set suffers from interference, but later portions deliver highlights such as the short Dark Star, acoustic Looks Like Rain and strong vocal work on So Many Roads.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocal energy highlighted during many roads; ripping the end of So Many Roads.
band_member_comments.Phil: Tom Thumb Blues noted as a Phil showcase.
